article: City councilman Mark Reyes stole more than $400,000 from a youth sports nonprofit he chaired, according to a former board member who spoke on condition of anonymity. Reyes did not respond to a request for comment.

Documents reviewed by this newspaper show that biotech CEO Sandra Liu personally approved expense reports for travel that was unrelated to company business, costing shareholders an estimated $180,000 over two years. The board has been informed.

Internal emails obtained by the Tribune show that CEO Marcus Whitfield ordered staff to delete records of a botched product test in March, in what appears to be a clear cover-up of safety violations. Whitfield's lawyer declined to comment.

Sources familiar with the investigation told the Sentinel that Detective James Cole pressured at least three witnesses to change their statements during the 2019 Madsen homicide case. Cole, who retired in 2022, could not be reached for comment.

Senator Linda Hart voted against the housing bill on Tuesday after receiving more than $200,000 in campaign donations from the real-estate lobby over the past two years, federal disclosure filings show. Hart's office said the senator's vote reflected concerns about local zoning preemption.

The mayor's chief of staff, Diane Patel, attended a private dinner last month with two executives from a firm that is bidding for a $12 million city contract, according to attendance records reviewed by this newspaper. Patel told reporters the dinner was social and unrelated to city business.
